response.justification: Bogdan has a genuinely relevant profile: current Staff-level work at Naver on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks maps directly to the AI-native engineering environment, and his time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency microservices) demonstrates credible distributed-systems depth. However, Python—a stated must-have—is entirely absent from the skills list and cannot be inferred from any role. There is also a conspicuous resume error: he lists 'MercadoLibre' as an employer for what the description makes clear is his own independent open-source GIS project, which raises a factual-accuracy concern that warrants direct clarification before advancing.
strengths: Active hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks at Naver—directly aligned with the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency architectures
Consistent Staff-level track record across multiple companies, plus open-source leadership and public talks indicating technical breadth and community engagement
concerns: Python is a listed must-have skill and is completely absent from the resume, skills section, and every role description—this gap must be resolved before an offer could realistically be made

The 'Founder, MercadoLibre' entry is a factual error (MercadoLibre is a publicly traded Latin American e-commerce giant); the description reveals it is an independent open-source project, undermining resume credibility
No mention of agile ceremonies, scrum, sprint cadence, or incident-response/on-call practices anywhere in the resume, leaving key must-have non-technical criteria unverified
